questions.question: Explain the two primary methods of I/O device communication (Programmed I/O and Interrupt-Driven I/O). Describe a simple scenario where you would choose one method over the other.
keyPoints: Definition and process of Programmed I/O (CPU polls the device)
Definition and process of Interrupt-Driven I/O (device signals the CPU)
Comparison of CPU utilization for both methods
Example scenario: Choose Interrupt-Driven I/O for efficient handling of sporadic events (e.g., keyboard press) to free the CPU for other tasks

questions.question: Describe the concept of memory-mapped I/O. What are its main advantages and disadvantages compared to port-mapped I/O?
keyPoints: Definition: I/O devices are accessed like memory locations using the same address bus
Advantage: Simpler instruction set, no special I/O instructions needed
Advantage: Can use all memory access instructions for I/O control
Disadvantage: Consumes physical memory address space
Disadvantage: Can make memory decoding more complex

questions.question: What is the primary role of device drivers in a computer system?
keyPoints: Act as a translation layer between the hardware device and the OS/application
Provide a standardized software interface to control a specific hardware device
Manage data transfer and error handling for the device
